mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and datasources using Kubernetes custom resources. It's ideal for users who prefer GitOps workflows or need to manage multiple Grafana instances efficiently.

steps.title: Install Grafana Operator using Helm
steps.description:
Deploy the Grafana Operator in your cluster using Helm. Ensure you have Helm installed and configured. Run the following command:
```bash
helm repo add grafana https://grafana.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm upgrade -i grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.0
```
Verify the installation by checking the operator's status:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n default -l app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana-operator
```

steps.title: Deploy Grafana Instance
steps.description:
Create a Grafana instance using a custom resource. Save the following YAML to a file named `grafana-instance.yaml`:
```yaml
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: secret
```
Apply the configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f grafana-instance.yaml
```
Check the status:
```bash
kubectl get grafana
```

steps.title: Deploy a Sample Dashboard
steps.description:
Create a sample dashboard using a custom resource. Save the following YAML to a file named `sample-dashboard.yaml`:
```yaml
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: GrafanaDashboard
metadata:
  name: sample-dashboard
spec:
  resyncPeriod: 30s
  instanceSelector:
    matchLabels:
      dashboards: "grafana"
  json: >
    {
      "title": "Simple Dashboard",
      "timezone": "browser",
      "refresh": "5s",
      "panels": [],
      "time": {
        "from": "now-6h",
        "to": "now"
      }
    }
```
Apply the dashboard configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f sample-dashboard.yaml
```

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure RBAC for Grafana. Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow access:
```yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: default
  name: grafana-role
rules:
- apiGroups: ["*"]
  resources: ["*"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "delete"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: grafana-role-binding
  namespace: default
subjects:
- kind: User
  name: your-username
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: grafana-role
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
```
Apply the RBAC configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f rbac-config.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
Remove the Grafana Operator and its resources. Run the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall grafana-operator
```
Clean up any remaining custom resources:
```bash
kubectl delete grafana --all
kubectl delete grafanadashboard --all
```
Verify that all resources are removed:
```bash
kubectl get all -l app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana-operator
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
To upgrade the Grafana Operator, first check the current version:
```bash
helm list
```
Backup your current Grafana instances and dashboards:
```bash
kubectl get grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml
kubectl get grafanadashboard -o yaml > dashboards-backup.yaml
```
Upgrade the operator using Helm:
```bash
helm upgrade grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.0
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n default -l app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana-operator
```

troubleshooting.title: Grafana Operator Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Grafana Operator pod is not starting, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n default
```
Common issues include missing permissions or incorrect configurations. Ensure that the service account has the necessary RBAC permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Grafana Instance Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access your Grafana instance, verify the service:
```bash
kubectl get svc -l app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana
```
Ensure that the service type is set to LoadBalancer or NodePort for external access.

troubleshooting.title: Dashboard Not Loading
troubleshooting.description:
If the dashboard is not loading, check the GrafanaDashboard resource:
```bash
kubectl describe grafanadashboard sample-dashboard
```
Look for errors in the JSON configuration or issues with the Grafana instance.

troubleshooting.title: RBAC Issues
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter RBAC errors, ensure that your Role and RoleBinding are correctly configured. Check the logs for permission denied errors and adjust the RBAC settings accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quota Exceeded
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive errors about resource quotas, check the resource usage:
```bash
kubectl describe quota
```
Adjust your resource requests/limits or increase the quota in your namespace.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for Grafana dashboards and data sources management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version for GrafanaDashboard.

versionNotes.changes: Added new features for better integration with Kubernetes RBAC.

resolution.summary: A successful setup includes a running Grafana Operator, accessible Grafana instances, and functioning dashboards. You should be able to manage Grafana resources using Kubernetes custom resources.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add grafana https://grafana.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm upgrade -i grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.0

apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: secret

apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: GrafanaDashboard
metadata:
  name: sample-dashboard
spec:
  resyncPeriod: 30s
  instanceSelector:
    matchLabels:
      dashboards: "grafana"
  json: >
    {
      "title": "Simple Dashboard",
      "timezone": "browser",
      "refresh": "5s",
      "panels": [],
      "time": {
        "from": "now-6h",
        "to": "now"
      }
    }

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher, and Helm installed for package management.
